text: NVIDIA B200 has 148 SMs (74 per die, dual-die NV-HBI unified). Expert-to-SM mapping limits for MoE.
Maximum 64 concurrent warps per SM for compute capability 10.0. Key occupancy ceiling.
64K 32-bit registers per SM. Register pressure trades off against occupancy.
Hard limit of 255 registers per thread. Exceeding causes spilling to local memory.
Maximum 32 thread blocks per SM for compute capability 10.0.
228 KB shared memory capacity per SM for compute capability 10.0. Tile size ceiling for GEMM kernels.
Maximum 227 KB shared memory per thread block (228KB minus 1KB CUDA reservation). Static allocations limited to 48KB, dynamic requires opt-in.
Shared memory carveout options are discrete, not continuous. Must choose from fixed set.

Tensor Memory (TMEM) is 256KB per SM, dedicated to tensor core operations. Structured as 512 columns x 128 lanes of 32-bit cells. Separates tensor core storage from registers. 16 TB/s read bandwidth, 8 TB/s write bandwidth per SM.
constraint: Only accessible via tcgen05 instructions, not via Triton directly

text: TMEM achieves optimal efficiency at 64x64 element tiles (4KB for FP8). Tiles <32x32 achieve only 45% of peak bandwidth. Tiles >128x128 trigger multi-phase transfers with 30% throughput reduction.
HBM3e theoretical ~8 TB/s bandwidth (measured ~7.48 TB/s). Roofline model ceiling for memory-bound kernels. HBM3e capacity up to 192 GB.
~64 MB L2 cache (8x8MB partitions on B200, 126MB on GB200). Critical for weight reuse in MoE — expert weights can be cached across token batches.
constraint: weight_tile_size should fit in L2 for reuse

text: 128-byte cache line size. All memory accesses should be aligned to cache lines for coalescing.
constraint: TMA alignment = 128 bits minimum, CUTLASS uses AlignmentA/B = 16 elements for FP8

text: Maximum cluster size 16 (non-portable) or 8 (portable) on B200. Clusters enable distributed shared memory for weight reuse across SMs via TMA multicast.
constraint: cluster_size must be power of 2, non-portable requires cudaFuncAttributeNonPortableClusterSizeAllowed

text: FP8 tensor core throughput: 3851 TFLOPS (96.3% of 4000 TFLOPS peak). 5th gen tensor cores use tcgen05.mma (warp-level, not warp-group). 11-cycle latency constant across tile sizes (2.9-11.6x lower than Hopper).
constraint: tensor cores are NOT the bottleneck — memory bandwidth and launch overhead are

text: FP4 tensor core throughput: 7702 TFLOPS (96.3% of peak). Supports MXFP4 (block-32, E8M0 scale) and NVFP4 (block-16, e4m3 scale).
constraint: FP4 requires hardware dequantization during MMA

text: tcgen05.mma has ~11 cycle latency, constant across tile sizes (11.0-11.4 cycles). FP32 accumulation halves throughput (1929 vs 964 TFLOPS for FP16). INT8 (3927 TOPS) slightly exceeds FP8.
constraint: FP32 accumulator cuts throughput 50%, prefer FP16 accum for inference

text: 5th gen NVLink at 1.8 TB/s. Transparent within CUDA model. Relevant for multi-GPU MoE with expert parallelism.
